Document:
# Wastelanders
Wastelanders are the independent survivors of the nuclear devastation — people who belong to no faction, answer to no flag, and make their living on the open Wasteland through skill, instinct, and personal code. They are the beating heart of every Fallout story: wanderers who walk into every situation without a predetermined role and walk out having shaped it.
No two wastelanders are alike. Former raiders who went straight. Travelers heading somewhere better. Drifters who have been walking since before they can remember. What they share is a refusal to be defined by anyone else's war.
## Philosophy
There is no single wastelander philosophy — that is the point. Independence and personal code are all that connects them. Some run by honor, others by caps, others by something harder to name. The Wasteland does not ask for justification. Only survival.
## Roles
[bold]Drifter[/bold]
[italic]In the Fallout universe:[/italic] The Drifter is the quintessential Fallout archetype — the protagonist of nearly every game in the series. Someone passing through who gets drawn into something larger. In lore, drifters represent freedom and unpredictability: no faction loyalty, no fixed agenda, and the ability to go anywhere and help or hurt anyone. The Courier of New Vegas is the archetype made playable.
[color=#a4885c]Your role:[/color] You are nobody's soldier and nobody's enemy by default. Make alliances, take contracts, pursue your own goals — or make someone else's goals your own. You are the wildcard this round needs.
[bold]Scavenger[/bold]
[italic]In the Fallout universe:[/italic] Scavengers are the Wasteland's recyclers — people who find value in wreckage, know which ruins are worth the risk, and understand that yesterday's garbage is today's survival gear. Scavengers in Fallout are foundational to the post-war economy: someone has to find the bullet casings, the copper wire, and the pre-war food before anyone else can use them.
[color=#a4885c]Your role:[/color] Explore, recover, and trade. You know what things are worth and where to look for them. The resources you bring back keep the local economy moving — and your own caps flowing.
[bold]Wasteland Medic[/bold]
[italic]In the Fallout universe:[/italic] Freelance healers occupy a uniquely trusted position across faction lines — their skills transcend politics because they are too valuable to antagonize. Like the Followers of the Apocalypse on a smaller scale, a skilled wasteland medic can walk into almost any settlement and be welcome. Characters like Arcade Gannon or independent docs throughout the series demonstrate the power of medical neutrality.
[color=#a4885c]Your role:[/color] Provide medical care to anyone who needs it — for caps, for favors, or out of principle. Your neutrality is a strength: you can go where soldiers cannot and be trusted where guns would get the door slammed.
## Relations
Wastelanders can work with or against any faction depending on personal goals and available caps. They are the wildcards of every round — their choices can shift the balance between factions in directions no one predicted.
